pairs.question: Who was George R. Gallagher and what role did he hold?
pairs.answer: George R. Gallagher was a judge on the District of Columbia Court of Appeals. He served from 1968 until his retirement in 2001, after being nominated by President Lyndon B. Johnson in 1967.

pairs.question: Where did George R. Gallagher grow up and where did he study law?
pairs.answer: Gallagher was born in New Haven, Connecticut, and moved to Washington, D.C., in the 1920s. He earned his law degree from the Columbus School of Law at the Catholic University of America after graduating from George Washington University.

pairs.question: What were some of George R. Gallagher's notable professional roles before joining the D.C. Court of Appeals?
pairs.answer: Before his judicial appointment, Gallagher worked at the U.S. Department of Justice Criminal Division, served as general counsel for the Subversive Activities Control Board in 1952, and practiced law privately starting in 1959. He also supported presidential campaigns as an advance man for John F. Kennedy and Lyndon B. Johnson.

pairs.question: What notable event involving Theodore R. Newman, Jr. occurred in 1980 related to George R. Gallagher?
pairs.answer: In 1980, Gallagher was part of a group of conservative judges led by Frank Q. Nebeker who unsuccessfully attempted to block Theodore R. Newman, Jr.'s reappointment as chief judge of the D.C. Court of Appeals.

pairs.question: What was George R. Gallagher's judicial reputation during his tenure?
pairs.answer: According to the Washington Post, Gallagher was regarded as a centrist judge throughout his time on the D.C. Court of Appeals.

pairs.question: When and why did George R. Gallagher leave the D.C. Court of Appeals?
pairs.answer: Gallagher took senior status in 1981 and retired from the court entirely in 2001. His departure was due to reaching the end of his judicial service rather than any disciplinary action.

pairs.question: Where did George R. Gallagher spend his later years and when did he pass away?
pairs.answer: After retiring, Gallagher lived on his farm in Comus, Maryland. He died there on February 4, 2007, at the age of 91.
